title: Reply to a mentor who declined a recommendation request
visible_context: The user asked a mentor for a recommendation, and the mentor declined because of timing. The user wants to respond graciously and preserve the relationship.
initial_user_message: My mentor said they can't write the recommendation right now. I want to reply well and not burn goodwill.
simulator_instructions: The user wants gratitude and maturity without sounding stiff.

title: Follow up with a speaker who canceled
visible_context: The user organizes an event and needs to reply to a speaker who canceled late. They want to keep the connection while clarifying logistics.
initial_user_message: A speaker canceled late and I need to respond without sounding annoyed even though this is inconvenient.
simulator_instructions: The user values diplomacy plus practical follow-through.

title: Reschedule a mentorship check-in
visible_context: The user needs to move a mentorship check-in because of an unexpected work meeting. They want to be respectful and keep the relationship warm.
initial_user_message: I need to move a mentor check-in and I don't want it to sound like I am taking them for granted.
simulator_instructions: The user wants warmth plus concrete rescheduling options.

title: Move a sales demo after internal delays
visible_context: The user needs to move a sales demo because their team is not ready. They want to preserve buyer confidence while rescheduling.
initial_user_message: I need to move a demo with a prospect and keep it from looking messy. Help me word it.
simulator_instructions: The user wants crisp, credible wording.

title: Password manager stopped autofilling
visible_context: The user's password manager stopped autofilling across browsers. They are annoyed but willing to troubleshoot if steps are clear.
initial_user_message: My password manager stopped autofilling and now everything is slow. Can you help me fix it without a bunch of fluff?
simulator_instructions: The user wants practical, ordered steps with minimal filler.

title: Microphone sounds distorted on calls
visible_context: The user gets reports that their microphone sounds distorted on calls. They need a clear plan to isolate whether the issue is software, device, or environment.
initial_user_message: People say my mic sounds distorted on calls. How should I troubleshoot it in a sane order?
simulator_instructions: The user appreciates a structured debugging path and will ask for more detail if steps feel shallow.

title: Pick a thank-you gift for a mentor manager
visible_context: The user wants a small thank-you gift for a mentor-like manager after a promotion. They want something thoughtful but still workplace-appropriate.
initial_user_message: I want a thank-you gift for a manager who really helped me, but I need it to stay professional.
task_success_criteria: give one or two strong options
simulator_instructions: The user wants a tasteful recommendation with reasoning.

title: Choose between a restful and social trip
visible_context: The user has limited time off and wants help deciding between a quiet cabin trip and a more social city weekend. They want help clarifying fit, not just pros and cons.
initial_user_message: I have a little time off and I am torn between a quiet trip and a city weekend. Can you help me choose based on what I actually need?
simulator_instructions: The user wants thoughtful personalization and may ask for a stronger recommendation.

title: Ask for a return exception on opened software
visible_context: The user bought software that does not work for their setup and wants to ask for a refund even though the product was already activated.
initial_user_message: I need to ask for a refund on software that is technically activated already. I want to make the case without sounding aggressive.
simulator_instructions: The user values credible, practical wording over emotional pleading.

title: Ask landlord for a short repair accommodation
visible_context: The user needs temporary accommodation while a repair issue makes part of the apartment hard to use. They want to ask clearly without escalating too hard.
initial_user_message: I need to ask my landlord for a short accommodation while a repair is unresolved. Help me phrase it firmly but reasonably.
simulator_instructions: The user wants firm, measured wording.

title: Reply to a teacher after a tense parent email
visible_context: The user, a teacher, received a tense email from a parent and wants to respond with calm and clarity. They need to stay empathetic without conceding things they cannot promise.
initial_user_message: A parent sent a heated email and I need to reply calmly. I want empathy but also boundaries.
simulator_instructions: The user wants a measured response with warmth and structure.

title: Message a friend after growing distant
visible_context: The user wants to reach out to a friend after months of distance following a hard patch. They want warmth without guilt-tripping or over-explaining.
initial_user_message: I want to message a friend I have been distant from. I want it to feel warm and honest, not heavy.
simulator_instructions: The user wants emotionally intelligent wording that still feels light enough to send.
